summary: Recent updates to the compositing and layers area of Firefox improve the management of visual layers in the browser, particularly during shutdown, which prevents errors by not trying to restore layers that were erased. There’s also enhanced support for Android with AImageReader, facilitating better image handling for applications. A small but notable adjustment allows a greater tolerance for pixel differences when dealing with window scrolling, improving visual accuracy. Additionally, improvements in code quality—such as modernizing type usage—contribute to better performance and maintainability of the browser's architecture. Overall, these changes are aimed at enhancing the stability and visual fidelity of the Firefox browsing experience, particularly in complex rendering scenarios. **This is crucial as it ensures smoother interactions and visuals for users, especially on mobile devices.**
